entry.title: Re: MM: nova regulamentação referente à tributação monofásica sobre combustíveis
entry.content:
Olá!

Existe alguma OSS Note ou tratativa para os casos de XML com ICMS monofásico e desconto (<vDesc>)?

Mesmo quando apenas o ICM7 é ativado, o saldo na MIRO não fecha com o total da NF-e. Além disso, o SAP calcula um imposto sobre este desconto na RB00 (J_1BNFLIN-NFDIS).

entry.title: Re: Aligning 3 SAP systems (CUSTOM, TEST, PRD) with the s...
entry.content: Hi Olga, thanks again for all your help! We understand that there is a standard SAP process that allows us to copy all master data from PRD into TEST/CUSTOM. What we would like to clarify is whether, as part of that process, the roles that currently exist in PRD are also copied. In other words, when we perform the PRD → TEST system copy, will the procedure include copying the PRD roles and their assignments into TEST, or is there a way to exclude them and preserve the existing TEST roles instead?

entry.title: Re: BANK RECONCILIATION METHOD IN SAP S4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content: forwarded again...

entry.title: Need logic for Custom report in SAP PM/EAM Equipment Performance Report
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

Could you please provide logic to fetch this Field in SAP PM/EAM for Custom report, 
If you could specifically help me with the Logic for 
" Actual Progress Field " 

Below is the logic I have used
Total Activity required I'm providing in the Input screen. 
Also the No. of Days Required = Total Activity Required ÷ (Equipment Capacity × Running Hours)
Expected Activity Progress = Equipment Capacity × Running HRS
For Running Hours I used the logic 
Pass ATNAM = RUNNING_HOURS to CABN and fetch ATINN value.
Pass MPOBJ = IE*Equipment Number* (EQUNR) and ATINN to table IMPTT to fetch the corresponding POINT.
Pass the derived POINT to table IMRG and fetch records within the selected date range (IDATE).
Filter records for each day and consider CDIFF (difference value) for the measuring point.
Group the records by IDATE (Activity Date) and calculate the SUM of CDIFF for each date.

Thanks!

Plant/Project Plant DescriptionEquipment NoEquipment DescriptionEquipment CapacityCapacity UnitRunning HRSActivity DateTotal activity requiredNo of days required to complete ActivityEstimated ProgressActual Progress

entry.title: Re: How to Upload Files to Document Service and Link Attachments to Leads or Opportunities

Hello, 

I need retrieve the binary (Base64) data directly in the response, how do I get this?

Best regards

entry.title: OT81 – Error message FIBL009 for cross country payments
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

with TC: OT81 we need to setup repetitive codes where paying company code and receiving company code are in different countries. The payment method to be used is only setup in the paying company code.

 

There’s error message FIBL009 indicating that the payment method is missing in the receiving company code. Despite the error message the repetitive code can be saved.

 

What is the purpose of the error message and how can it be omitted?

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: How to Upload Files to Document Service and Link Attachments to Leads or Opportunities
entry.content:
@S002288386733 
you will have to pass your document UUID to the below response to reterive base64 encoded content

GET https://example.crm.cloud.sap/sap/c4c/api/v1/document-service/document/<documentUUID>

entry.title: Re: I want to restrict the edit functionality after the i...
entry.content:
Hello!

The requirement cannot be solved through role configuration alone. The solution requires two layers working together: a clean role design and a custom logic rule that enforces the restriction at the application level based on document status.

 

Business Role Redesign:

The goal here is to ensure the invoice initiator's role carries only the access their job function genuinely requires.

Identify the relevant business catalogs -> open the sap Fiori apps reference library and look up the apps your initiators use.

In the Maintain Business Roles app, create a copy of the role currently assigned to invoice initiators. Review and tighten Write access.

This step alone will not block editing of posted documents, because write access is not document-status-aware.

Lastly assign the new role to initiator users -> replace the existing role assignment with the new custom role for all invoice initiators.

 

Custom Logic

This is where the actual restriction is enforced. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a standard extensibility framework called Custom Logic (search for app Custom Logic).

Search within Custom Logic for BAdIs related to supplier invoice processing, specifically ones that fire on a save or change event.Here implement the restriction rule.

The logic to be implemented does three things in sequence:

- Check whether the invoice document already has a posted status, meaning an FI accounting document has been generated against it.

- Check whether the user currently attempting the change is the original creator of the document.

- If both conditions are true, raise an error message that prevents the save and clearly tells the user to use a credit memo or reversal instead.

If the requirement is to block editing for all users, not just the initiator, the logic can be simplified to check only the document status, regardless of who the current user is.

 

If the specific BAdI needed for this restriction may not be available in the Custom Logic app. If that happens, the recommended path is to raise an SAP support and ask SAP to confirm the supported extensibility point for enforcing edit restrictions on posted supplier invoices in the Public Cloud edition.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Cerrado 2026
entry.content: Presença garantida. Ótimo evento à todos!

entry.title: Re: Error while closing Asset Fiscal Year where Fiscal ye...
entry.content: Final Resolution - We posted all previous depreciation in Previous posting period (need to open that period) then closed previous periods. Balance carry forward to current period.

entry.title: Re: INSERT errors out
entry.content:
I have no idea what sp_F is nor what it has to do with the original question of a failed insert (due to a column not allowing NULLs).

-------------------

Have you solved the original issue (failing insert due to column not allowing NULLs)?

If the answer is 'yes' and you're now running into a new issue then I'd suggest asking a new question.

If the answer is 'no' then could you please provide answers to my previous questions.

entry.title: Re: Embedded vs Standalone GRC Installation / Approach
entry.content:
No no. It isnt… but its way cheaper than standalone. 
Licensing cost varies client to client.

but from my experience ..for my client, the embedded one costed around 20% of standalone RISE installation of GRC.

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core: Deployment and Configuration Errors and How to Fix Them (Part 2 of 2)
entry.content:
Hi @DEEPA_DORAIRAJ 

File Input issue in orchestration API

We need to pass file-input in the orchestration API. But the SDK suggests the prompt to be passed as messages in chat completion. Due to this we had to hardcode the prompt in the code . Providing the prompt in the template is not working and giving error. Is there any way we can create a template for the prompt and pass that in the file input call. 
Provided the  example below of the usage. Looking for a way we can maintain the user or at least the system prompt as prompt template. Right now, neither of them works in prompt template.

 const client = new OrchestrationClient(
          {
            promptTemplating: {
              model: { name: 'anthropic--claude-4.5-sonnet', params: { n: 1, temperature: 0 } },
              prompt: {
                template: [
                  {
                    role: 'system',
                    content:
                      'You are a document text extraction expert. Extract the complete text from the attached PDF file(s) exactly as it appears.\n\n' +
                      'Return ONLY a JSON array — no explanation, no markdown.\n\n' +
                      'The user message lists the fileId for each file. Use those exact values.\n\n' +
                      'Format: [{ "fileId": "<id>", "content": "<full extracted text>" }]',
                  },
                ],
              },
            },
          },
          { resourceGroup: AI_TEMPLATES.RESOURCE_GROUP },
          { destinationName: AI_TEMPLATES.DESTINATION }
        );

        // Build an explicit ordered ID list so the LLM knows exactly which fileId
        // to return for each attached PDF. The filename property on the file block is a
        
        const fileIdList = lcFiles
          .map((file, i) => `File ${i + 1}: fileId="${String(file.ID)}"`)
          .join(', ');

        const response = await client.chatCompletion({
          messages: [
            {
              role: 'user', // file type only supported in 'user' role
              content: [
                {
                  type: 'text',
                  text: `Files (use these exact fileId values in your JSON response): ${fileIdList}`, // content is in the file blocks below
                },
                ...fileContentBlocks,
              ],
            },
          ],
        });

SAP AI Core

entry.title: Guided Work Instructions not part of POD 2.0
entry.content:
We are currently exploring the Guided Steps - POD and Guided Steps - Production Process plugins. These plugins are available in the POD Designer application (POD 1.0)

We are planning to implement POD 2.0. In the Manage POD 2.0 applications, we tried adding the Guided Steps - POD and Guided Steps Production Process plugins, but the plugins are not available in POD 2.0

We tested this using the Blank POD, Operation Activity POD, Order POD, and Work Center POD templates, but the plugins still do not appear.

entry.title: Re: Promotion Management Job Failure in 4.1
entry.content: Add full control rights to temprary storage in CMC to resolve the issue

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core: Deployment and Configuration Errors and How to Fix Them (Part 2 of 2)
entry.content:
hi @KM11 

Good question and a known limitation worth documenting clearly.

The root cause is that the Orchestration SDK's prompt template system currently doesn't support file-type content blocks in the template definition itself — templates are designed for text-based message structures. File inputs using the filecontent type must be passed at runtime in the chatCompletion() call, specifically in the user role messages, which is exactly what your implementation is doing.

For the system prompt specifically — your current approach of hardcoding it in the promptTemplating.prompt.template array is actually the correct pattern. The system prompt doesn't need to be dynamic for file extraction use cases since it's instructional rather than data-driven. What you're hitting is that you can't combine a template-defined system prompt with a runtime file input in the same template reference — the SDK treats them as separate concerns.

The cleanest workaround for maintaining the system prompt as a configurable template while still passing file inputs at runtime:

Define the system prompt in a separate configuration constant or environment variable rather than a prompt template stored in AI Core. At runtime, construct the full message array combining your configured system prompt with the dynamic user message and file content blocks — exactly as your current code does. This gives you the maintainability of a template without fighting the SDK's file input constraints.

const SYSTEM_PROMPT = process.env.EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT || 
  'You are a document text extraction expert...';

const response = await client.chatCompletion({
  messages: [
    { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },
    { role: 'user', content: [
        { type: 'text', text: `Files: ${fileIdList}` },
        ...fileContentBlocks
    ]}
  ]
});

This is a limitation worth raising with SAP support under component CA-ML-AIP — file input support in prompt templates is a reasonable feature request and documenting it helps the product team prioritize it.

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management enabled
entry.content:
Hi,

For the recommendation on when to use a new position, you may refer to the following IDP, chapter 6.7.5, Updating Existing Position Vs. Using a New/ Vacant Position:

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Position Management: Design Considerations and Recommendations (https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/successfactors/implementation-design-principles)

Technically, both scenarios are possible (publish from Compensation and reuse of the same position), with synchronization from Job Information to Position. For this topic, please refer to the Position Transfer and Position Reclassification

Depending on the customer process, reusing the position, even when there is a change in Job Classification and/or Pay Grade may not be an issue. If, for instance, Pay Grade is relevant for headcount planning, it may be a problem, because the "implicit cost" of the position will be different.

Thanks,

Takashi

entry.title: Re: Document Migration: Understanding User Difference Between Onboarding 1.0 and BizX
entry.content:

We are postponing the UI removal to avoid migration disruption. It will not take place on May 15th.

" Data will not be deleted immediately after 15th May. You can continue to access ONB 1.0 application and the data. Please prioritize your ONB 1.0 transition activity"

Best regards,
Jacson
SAP Support

entry.title: ‌🇮🇳‌ Webinar: GSTR-1A for India GST in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
You are invited to join a 60-minute webinar covering recent enhancements for India GST in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

In this session, we will introduce and demonstrate the following key functionalities:

  • GSTR-1A
  • GSTR-3B tax liability break-up

Both capabilities have been released as part of ongoing improvements for India GST reporting within the solution.

The webinar will provide an overview of these updates, along with a guided walkthrough to help you understand their scope and business relevance.

REGISTER NOW

Want to connect with others and learn more about SAP Document and Reporting Compliance topics in India? Join the India - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Workzone group.

entry.title: Re: Cost Optimization in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @appel_solar_dk Thanks Kristian for your feedback. Yes I do agree, we should not outright reduce based on resource usage and we did not do that either, we worked with our team and reduced the size of tenant overall but not exactly to its usage. Yes we always need to consider business impact with whatever sizing decision you take to reduce infra cost.

entry.title: How do you populate the import parameter /1BCDWB/DOCXML in your sap adobe form?
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

How do you populate the import parameter /1BCDWB/DOCXML in your sap adobe form?

I am testing it via tcode SFP and i saw this import parameter /1BCDWB/DOCXML and I am not sure how to correctly populate it.

Please help by providing a sample source code.

Thanks,

Newguycloud

entry.title: To-Do option in FIORI, but under the "My Home Page" settings
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We are trying to enable the To-Do option in FIORI, but under the "My Home Page" settings (which appears grayed out), we are unable to "turn on" this option. 

Some users are able to enable it, while others are not.

Could you help us identify what needs to be done or how to enable this option in SAP FIORI?

I’ve attached an image of the grayed-out option for clarity.

I look forward to your prompt response.

entry.title: Re: How do you populate the import parameter /1BCDWB/DOCXML in your sap adobe form?
entry.content: Generally you would fill it with the appropriate language and country for locale handling and you might fill additional parameters based on the scenario.

entry.title: ‌🇲🇽‌ Webinar: ASUG Mexico Webinar SIG HCM/SFSF/Payroll
entry.content:
As amendments to the Federal Labor Law on reduced working hours take shape in Mexico, organisations must adapt while ensuring compliance, control, and operational continuity.

This session outlines how SAP Workforce solutions, SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking, and SAP Time Evaluation can support schedule adjustments, time management, and compliance requirements, while enabling informed decision-making through enhanced visibility.

Agenda
1. Current context of changes in the Federal Labor Law
2. Support options within SAP landscapes

  • SAP Time & Attendance by SAP Workforce
  • SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking
  • SAP Time Evaluation

Speakers
Julio Suarez, SAP LAC HCM Solution Advisory I
Luis Francisco Castro, SAP LAC HCM Sol Advisory Brazil Ext
Hugo Rodriguez, SAP GPM Brazil

For further details and to register, please use the following link.

Are you interested in learning more about SAP ERP Human Capital Management topics in Mexico and collaborate with SAP experts? Join the Mexico - Human Capital Management Localization regional collaboration group.

entry.title: Re: Your request is under clarification by export control.
entry.content: Hi Tsvetinka, we finally were ready and tried to launch again but getting the same message again. We appreciate the help!

entry.title: Warehouse and Transportation Synchronization in SAP S/4HANA Using SAP EWM and TM
entry.content:
Abstract & Introduction

During SAP TM and SAP EWM integration projects, many consultants are able to configure basic transportation planning and warehouse execution processes individually, but struggle when integrating both systems together in real-time business scenarios. as per my previous implementations, I observed that transportation planning worked correctly in SAP TM, while Transportation Units, loading instructions, freight settlement integration, or warehouse execution synchronization failed because of small missing integration configurations.

Biggest challenges in SAP TM and EWM integration is like the process spans multiple components including SD, MM, TM, EWM, and FI. Although SAP standard documentation explains individual configuration nodes, it does not clearly explain the sequence of integration dependencies, organizational assignments, Freight Unit formation logic, or the practical impact of missing configurations during end-to-end execution and settlement testing.

This article was written from a practitioner perspective to help my fellow consultants understand not only the configuration activities, but also the operational flow and common integration dependencies involved in real project environments.  

In this document, simulating a scenario starting from Sales to delivery of the goods to final customer and settlement of freight cost. Herein SAP MM, SD, EWM, TM & FI should be well integrated however focus is majorly on SAP EWM and TM.

(SD- Sales and distribution, MM-material management, TM-Transport management, EWM-Extended warehouse management, FI-Finance)

Fig1: SAP SD + SAP TM – Operational process steps

Fig2: SAP TM + SAP EWM – Operational process steps

Fig3: SAP EWM + SAP TM – Operational process steps

Fig4: SAP TM + SAP MM - Operational process steps

Customization

#1 Assignment of location (1003) to EWM target system/Warehouse number (/SCMTMS/LOC3):

It is used to manage transportation location (shipping point) that is required in transportation planning and logistics execution. The assignment of location 1003 to the EWM target system and warehouse number in /SCMTMS/LOC3 ensures that SAP TM can correctly identify the EWM warehouse responsible for transportation execution activities such as loading, unloading, and warehouse processing.

Sometimes, I have seen people use other location 1001 or 1002 but they miss 1003, in that they struggle later while unit testing. 

#2 Define Integration profile:

It is used to control, how TM documents and EWM documents communicate with each other. It clearly says that how loading/unloading instructions to EWM. This also includes Transportation unit in loading/unloading instructions.

SPRO -> TM-> Integration -> Define EWM Integration Profile

If this configuration is not done well, the loading instruction will not go to EWM and transportation unit will not be populated in SAP EWM with assigned deliveries, so it will take lot to struggle for consultant to identify the root cause.

#3 Link Freight Order (REFO) with EWM integration profile:

SPRO->TM->Freight order management->Freight Order->Define freight order type

This configuration is used to create and control different types of Freight Orders and make a link between freight order and EWM integration profile. With this integration only, loading/unloading info go to EWM for that particular Fright order types.

#4 Link between packaging material and Means of transport: /SCWM/PM_MTR:

This defines which packaging materials are allowed or suitable for specific means of transport in SAP EWM and TM integration. This is particularly used for transportation planning like what type of transport it would be like Truck, Airplane, container etc.

#5 Activate PPF message: SPPFC (choose /SCWM/SHP_REV and /SCMWM/TU): 

This is the backbone of SAP EWM and TM integration, wherein action definitions need to be activated for major action profile. Action Definition Activation means enabling an automatic SAP system action so it can execute during a business process based on defined conditions.

Example: 

Final Action = Send Freight Order to carrier
when this will Trigger = On Save
When Condition is fulfilled = Carrier assigned
if only Activation = YES, then only it will be automatically

This configuration should NOT be missed.

#6 Map Purchase organization to ERP account assignment: 

This is also one of the critical configurations, which links SAP TM purchasing organizations with ERP accounting structures so freight procurement and settlement costs can post correctly into ERP finance. 

If you miss or wrongly assign the cost center/Internal order then Service PO creation will be failed and accounting postings may go wrong, and finally this will impact the settlement. T

SPRO->Transport Management->Settlement->Forwarding Settlement->Setting for posting Intracompany settlements->Assign Internal order/Cost center to sender organization

#7 Organization unit:

Need to configure the organization elements specially purchase organization and purchasing groups. This tell, which department/company/team is managing this whole transportation process. Like who owns Transportation Planning or Purchasing & Carrier Procurement etc.

SPRO->Transport Management->Master data->Organizational Management->Organizational model->Create organizational model

Created org unit for my purchase organization REPO and purchasing group called RPG. One more important is the sequence, first BP and then create organization unit by assigning BP and configuration entity(Purchase org/Group).

#8 Control parameters:

This parameter control, which document will be sent(integrate) to EWM, whether Sales order or delivery.

In this document, however I am sending Delivery document to TM for Integration, as selected Outbound in below configuration.

SPRO->Transport Management->Integration->Logistics Integration->Internal TM component Integration->Define document for document integration

#9 Logistics Integration profile:

This tells SAP TM how transportation requirements should be converted into Freight Units for planning and execution. This is a part of Freight unit formation and also includes the freight unit building rule. Sequence is Freight unit building rule (FUBR) and then logistics integration profile by assigning the FUBR. Moreover, FUBR dictate how deliveries will be grouped into fright unit using same delivery route, same delivery date etc.

SPRO->Transport Management->Integration->Logistics Integration->Internal TM component Integration->Define logistics integration profile for SD,MM,LE

#10 Determine Control key and Logistics Integration profile:

SPRO->Transport Management->Integration->Logistics Integration->Internal TM component Integration->Define transportation relevance of delivery documents

This configuration node fetches the control key and Logistics integration profile for the combination of shipping point and Delivery type.

Master data

#1 Business Partner(BP):

Vendor as a Plant:

Created Vendor/BP 47/600135 for plant and assigned to plant.

Assigned BP/Vendor to vendor

Carrier:

A carrier is the transportation company that moves goods, and SAP stores it as a Business Partner master record. Below RE_CA_TM carrier was created using Tcode BP with prescribed roles, especially Carrier profile "CRM010"

#2 Resource Master:

Resource Master in SAP TM represents the physical transportation equipment or vehicle used to execute transportation activities and shipment planning.

Example: one company has 100 trucks then each truck is created as a Resource Master. Created truck RE_10_TK_LTL as a resource.

End to end Transactions

#1 Sales order(VA01): 

Created Sales order # #1960 for material RE-FUEL-TANK for customer(RE_CU_TM).

 

#2 Outbound Delivery(VL01N):

Crated outbound delivery for above sales order and a new tab "TM Status. This signifies SAP S4HANA delivery is well integrated with SAP TM. Moreover, this Tab is coming, during Delivery creation (not Sales order creation), because integration made at delivery level.  Just for information please check above mentioned node "Define document for document integration"

 

If you select the "TM Status", then you can see Freight unit which is first stage of Transport Management.

#3 Freight Unit:

A Freight Unit is A transportation demand, which clears what to transport, from where, to where, quantity and finally delivery dates.

Under this step, we are opening the Freight unit (using web based TM system), which is associated with particular delivery.

Goto Planning tab->Select the Freight unit Worklist

#4 Freight order:

A Freight Order is actual transportation execution document, which is used for plan the truck, assigning carrier, route execution and shipment tracking. so a freight order contains Carrier, Vehicle, Source Location, Destination, Pickup Date, Freight Units etc.

Under this step, creating Freight order by using below highlighted path.

Assigning Truck resource and Carrier 

#5 Displaying Freight order:

Freight order contains Carrier, Vehicle, Source Location, Destination, Pickup Date, Freight Units etc. Below Freight order screen shot shows the document flow, includes Freight unit, Outbound delivery and sales order. It is also showing freight amount, as applicable.

#6 Consignment order:

consignment order is a business document used to group multiple transportation requirements (freight units) based on shared criteria such as source/destination location, delivery date/time, and business partner. It acts as a communication tool between the supplier (consignor) and the customer (consignee), indicating which products will be delivered together and when.

Below, manually creating the consignment order through Freight order

#7 Send loading Instructions to EWM

Under this step, sending loading instructions to EWM, which will help to send the Freight order to be distributed to Transportation unit in SAP-TM. 

#8 Send Instructions:

This step is used to process the PPF action but can also schedule a background job for processing the PPF actions. To make this happen immediately, executed this step.

#9 Check Transportation unit:

This used to check whether Transportation unit is created (Same as Freight order). If it is not populating that means, PPF actions in previous step not executed successfully.

#10 Creation and confirmation of Warehouse task:

Now Transportation unit is created with assigned delivery, and this step will do warehouse task creation process. This step will be done in EWM using the tcode /SCWM/TU

#11 Warehouse task creation(Picking process): 

This step is the part of picking process, here warehouse task is being created against outbound delivery order. Here warehouse task is creating manually under tcode /SCWM/PRDO

Warehouse order #6 created.

#12 Confirming Warehouse task/Order

As after creation the warehouse task/order, immediate step is to confirm the warehouse task. which can be done using scanner(RFUI) but here confirming the same, using the manual option.

#13 Loading + Goods Issue completion (EWM):

Once material is picked and next step is loading the material in truck and complete the goods issues process. In this step, completing loading and goods issue at Transportation unit level (now this information will go to SAP TM, and goods issue information will go to S4HANA for Inventory updates).

#14 Freight settlement document

In SAP Transportation Management (TM), a freight settlement document (FSD) is essential for managing transportation costs and facilitating invoice verification with suppliers or carriers. other words Freight Settlement Document is the document used to calculate and settle transportation costs with the carrier. this contains Freight charges, Taxes, Carrier details, Shipment reference and Cost calculation.

#15 Settlement:

Settlement Process in SAP TM is the process of calculating transportation charges, creating freight settlement documents, and sending the costs to finance for carrier payment. Transaction wise, it will create service purchase order and service entry sheet automatically (if SAP TM and SAP MM+ SAP FI are integrated well).

In case of error, it will not create service PO or service entry sheet and finally FSD will not be invoiced.

Conclusion

In real SAP projects, many integration issues do not originate from core transportation planning itself, but from missing integration dependencies between TM, EWM, MM, and FI configurations. May be a small configuration gaps such as incomplete PPF activation, incorrect purchasing organization mapping, missing warehouse-location assignments, or improperly maintained organizational elements can significantly impact Transportation Unit creation, warehouse execution, freight settlement, and settlement postings. Similarly, approaches may vary depending on embedded or decentralized EWM landscapes, customer-specific transportation processes, warehouse automation requirements, and freight settlement strategies.

Apart from normal configuration steps, my fellow consultants should focus on understanding the integration dependencies, business process sequencing, communication flow between systems, and operational impact of each configuration object. 

 

Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, CIMCORP etc. He worked on various type of project flavors like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

entry.title: Activation of duplicate Business Partner check using the same Tax ID (CPF and CNPJ)
entry.content: I would like to understand the configuration required to activate the validation feature that prevents the creation of a Business Partner with the same CPF or CNPJ in the Brazil localization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Re: Exploring SAP BTP with Trial Accounts and Free Tiers
entry.content:
Hi @SriPatneedi 

The "Configure" option or "APIs" tab is missing because the API Management capability has not been activated inside your SAP Integration Suite tenant. Even if your user account holds all the correct global administrator and role collections, capabilities inside the SAP Integration Suite must be explicitly enabled before their respective user interfaces appear.

Enable it and check, it should work for you.

Thanks

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: Custom field not coming in PO print xml structure
entry.content: Thanks Dawid for your detailed explanation.

entry.title: How to check the field values for FPAYHX-REF used in payment medium
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

I am using standard CGI_CT DME structure for payment medium. in some of the nodes the soure filed is mapped as FPAYHX-REF  user defined field (eg FPAYHX-REF07, FPAYHX-REF05). but we cannot find the exact field in the system.

SO can you support to find the standard fields mapped for FPAYHX-REF.

Regards 

Kalee

entry.title: Watch the SAP Fieldglass 2026.05 Release Highlights Video Series
entry.content:
The SAP Fieldglass 2026.05 Release Highlights video series is now available. This series of 18 short, focused videos showcase some of the key innovations delivered in the release, with each video providing a quick overview and demo of the release item.

The videos have been grouped into four playlists, so you can easily choose which ones you want to watch:

  • Contingent Labor
  • Services Procurement
  • UX and Universal Updates
  • Supplier Updates

Visit the SAP Fieldglass SAP Community page for more release resources, including the SAP Fieldglass 2026.05 Release - Spotlight Features Guide.

entry.title: Re: Document Migration: Understanding User Difference Between Onboarding 1.0 and BizX
entry.content: @JacsonMiranda Thank you for explaining what to expect. This is great news to hear.

entry.title: ‌🇲🇽‌ SAP Customer Success Day LAC - Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Join us at the SAP Customer Success Day LAC - Cloud ERP to discover how SAP's localized solutions can help your company with local regulations and legal requirements in Mexico.

You will also have the opportunity to engage with our product managers in the region and gain insights into how SAP localized solutions can help streamline your local and international business operations. 

Speakers:
Paulina Sotomayor, GS GPM Brazil, SAP
Monica Marcellin, GS GPM Brazil, SAP 

REGISTER NOW

Are you interested in learning more from SAP experts? Join the regional Work Zone groups in Mexico to stay up to date:

  • Mexico - Human Capital Management Localization
  • Mexico — SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 - Release highlights of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content: We only want HR admins to do this step: 3- Cancel Form I-9 and E-Verify Processes from the New Hire Details page is there a way to disable/permission?

entry.title: Re: How to check the field values for FPAYHX-REF used in...
entry.content:
You can check the method 'GENERIC_CALL' of class 'CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_GENERIC' to get the SAP logic for filling the REF fields.  Anything customer-specific is managed by configuring event 06 and providing a FM with the necessary logic (a handful of sample FMs exist for reference) for those fields starting with 'ZREF'.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: BOM - Bill Of Material report
entry.content: This is great! any way to order by Father or "Assembly" so that the query returns all of the components in order by assembly? Thanks

entry.title: Information Error "Follow-up action EWM_AP not allowed for Usage L"
entry.content:
Hi,

We are having QM-EWM integration.

While working on Partial Usage Decision we are entering partial Quantity & Partial usage decision codes at "EWM Inspection" tab. Stocks gets posted as per partial usage decision codes and distributed to respective EWM bins.

For the sake of changing Inspection lot, we need to pass & Save UD Code at "Usage Decision" header level. WHile click on SAVE getting information popup "Follow-up action EWM_AP not allowed for Usage L". Client does not want to see this pop-up during transaction.

Kindly help us with suitable suggestions to implement.

Thank You,

Dhrumit J Shah

entry.title: Re: Assistance With Crystal Report With Barcodes
entry.content: I inherited this system. How do I find/download/install that library?

entry.title: Debug command "Suspend" in Eclipse ADT
entry.content:
I'm wondering what the debug command "Suspend" is for, when it can be selected, when to use it. Each time I look at the button, it's inactive.

NB:

  • The command "Suspend" didn't exist in the "old/new" SAP GUI backend debugger.
  • I couldn't find any explanation in the SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/navigating-in-debugger?utm_source=chatgpt.com&locale=en-US 

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Debug command "Suspend" in Eclipse ADT
entry.content:
The command "Suspend" can be selected in the Eclipse ADT debugger when the debugger is "stepping", i.e. when the debugger has been started (i.e. it has stopped on a first break point) and the code is currently running (i.e. when continuing after a break point and before the next break point, if any).

The command "Suspend" suspends the execution as soon as possible (technical limitations like SQL running, minimal amount of time), anywhere in the code.

One reason to use it is when the code runs for a long time without pausing at one of your breakpoints, and you suspect a performance issue or an endless loop.

One frequent case is that a background job is running for a long time and has to be debugged for analyzing:

  • Start SM51 via Eclipse ADT, select the workprocess and then the menu Process > Program > Debug
  • The debugger starts anywhere in the code
  • You can then check what happens, add breakpoints to better analyze
  • If you continue the job and your breakpoints are still not reached, you may run the command "Suspend" to suspend the execution and start debugging.

Remarks about the "old/new" SAP GUI backend debugger:

  • The command equivalent to "Suspend" was quite exhausting: SM51 > Select the workprocess > menu Process > Program > Debug, and often did not work the second and next times (no idea why, maybe one workaround was to continue the execution by stopping the backend debugger via the menu Debugger > Exit (Application continues running)).
  • "Suspend" was missing because, if I'm not wrong, it was technically impossible because only one process could be active at a moment, either the debugged process or the debuggee process, i.e. when the debugged process was running it was not possible to click a debug option in the debuggee process as its SAP GUI window was inactive.

entry.title: Re: How Do You Decide Your SAP S/4HANA Migration Path?
entry.content: Valuable information and guidance in addressing key areas in all 9 sections angle in the decision-making process.  Thank you

entry.title: Re: BADI for Mass Schedule Maintenance Plans
entry.content:
Email Notification for Preventive Maintenance Orders (S/4HANA Cloud 2602)

There are two approaches to send email notifications when preventive maintenance orders are automatically generated via IP10/IP30:

Option 1: Flexible Workflow for Maintenance Orders (Recommended)

Use Fiori app F4989 – Flexible Workflow for Maintenance Orders.

Configuration approach:

  • Define global preconditions such as:

    • Order Type (preventive)
    • Maintenance Plan is not empty (ensures trigger from IP10/IP30)
    • Planning Plant / Planner Group (optional)
  • Add a Notification Only step:
    • Assign recipients (e.g., partner on order, planner group, person responsible, or specific users)
    • Set execution to auto complete in My Inbox
  • Email setup:
    • Use Fiori app Manage Email Templates
    • Copy standard template:  SWT_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS
    • Follow naming convention: YY_02000019_CRT_10
    • Add Subject: Alert: Maintenance Order {{OrderNumber}} from PM is generated
    • Add Body: Maintenance Order {{OrderNumber}} has been generated via Maintenance Plan for Technical Object {{TechnicalObject}}. Please review your technical log.
  • Prerequisites:
    • User email maintained in Maintain Business User / Manage Business Partner
    • Preventive order type assigned to Phase Model Profile (workflow trigger based on status change to Approval)

Option 2: BAdI-Based Enhancement (Advanced / Extension Approach)

For more ABAP logic, use BADI_EAM_MO_CH_BEF_SAVE with ABAP Cloud extensibility.

Approach:

  • Trigger logic in BAdI when:

    • Order Type (preventive)
    • Maintenance Plan is not empty (ensures trigger from IP10/IP30)
    • Planning Plant / Planner Group (optional)

  • Send email using: CL_BCS_MAIL_MESSAGE

  • Key steps:

    • Key steps: Set sender using .set_sender()
    • Add recipients dynamically via .add_recipient() (e.g., from I_MaintenanceOrder CDS view)
    • Define subject using .set_subject()
    • Send asynchronously using .send_asynchronous() (to avoid transactional impact)

Recommendation

  • Use Flexible Workflow (Option 1) for a standard, clean core, and configuration-driven solution
  • Use BAdI (Option 2) only if additional logic or flexibility is required beyond workflow capabilities

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Issue of adding serial numbers to sales return delive...
entry.content:
The customer returns delivery API listed here - https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_CUSTOMER_RETURN_DELIVERY_SRV_0002/path/post_A_ReturnsDeliveryHeader includes the capability for inclusion of serial numbers under the maintenance object list portion of the schema.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: TVE1 table Maintenance Generator, possible?

entry.title: Re: INSERT errors out
entry.content:
@MarkAParsons,

In your first reply you wrote::

 

> What was tempdb's setting for 'allow nulls by default' at the time the table was created?  Had you set 'allow nulls by default' to 'false' at some time in the past?

 

I didn't execute such a command. I only executed it for my DB. That's why in my reply I asked if I have to run it for the "tempdb" as well and also run every single option change in both.

Sorry for the confusion.

 

Let me try to run it for the "tempdb" as well and see what will happen.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: ILX manager view: updated features: How to manage access
entry.content:
Hi @janaernouts2 

I don't think the application has a permission that controls the option 'View Curricula Status'. Looking at a user's role (such as DEFAULT USER) and expanding the 'My Team' section, there is no permission related to the 'View Curricula Status' button from the Team View page.

Alternatively, to suggest a different system behavior, it would be possible to have an enhancement request created for it (as per KBA 2090228), where the Learning Product Management team can further review and assess the requested functionality to be considered in a future release.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Length of consolidation unit in Group reporting
entry.content: you can adjust the length of the field but question is on the impact of other tables/fields. Alternatively you can create a new dimension but then you need to ensure all of your consolidation reporting includes this new dimension.

entry.title: Re: Assessment Restriction Requirements in SuccessFactors LMS
entry.content:
Hi @ramyamula69 

By these requirements, you mean to restrict actions the users can take while answering assessments from the user side?

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: LMS alternate managers cannot see data in Story Reports
entry.content:
Hi @briangarcia 

If a user is configured as someone's Alternate Manager within LMS, I think the Story report should display the data. To check if a user has an alternate manager:

  1. Navigate to Learning Administration > People > Users >
  2. Search for the user for which the data needs to be fetched in a Story report (e.g., user A) >
  3. At the Actions button, click on 'Assign Alternate Managers' >
  4. At the wizard that opens up, the alternate managers and/or HRBP assigned to the user A will be listed >
  5. If user A has an alternate manager (e.g., B), then I think B should be able to see data from user A in the Story report.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: INSERT errors out
entry.content:
sp_dboption is used to change the settings for a single database.

If you need multiple databases to have their settings changed then you need to run sp_dboption once for each database in question.

NOTE: If you're using multiple tempdb's, and they are being assigned to users in a round-robin(ish) approach, then you should insure all tempdbs have been configured the same.

entry.title: Diesel consumption measurement document in SAP PM
entry.content:
I'm having trouble tracking diesel consumption for a specific plant. The transaction used for consumption is IFCU. The sequence is to create the equipment, create the metering points, and then a refueling order. When trying to record the deduction, the tab for entering the results remains closed. See image below: 

Here is an image of the equipment that provides the consumption figures. This one is from a different plant:

  

 Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Re: INSERT errors out
entry.content: @MarkAParsons, it looks like I have to run "sp_dboption tempdb, 'allow nulls by default', 'true'" AND "sp_dboption <myDB>, 'allow nulls by default', 'true'" in order to allow NULLS to be inserted. Thx. Now I presume that whenever I need to run "sp_dboption" I will have to run it for both my own db and the "tempdb".

entry.title: Intern Conversion to Regular/ FT Employee
entry.content: I would like to do some benchmarking on how other companies that use ONB 2.0 and Employee Central are handling intern conversions with no break in service. Since implementing ONB 2.0 this has been a pain point for us. It was not an issue in ONB 1.0 since it was not inherently tied to PC, we could control the timing of processing, etc.

entry.title: Audit Report for Business Roles Including UI Switches in SAP C4C
entry.content:
Hello SAP C4C Experts,


I am currently trying to build a complete mapping report in SAP Sales Cloud (C4C) between:
-Business Roles
-Work Centers / Work Center Views
-Access Restrictions / Access Rights
-UI Switches
The issue is that I could not find a standard report or data source that combines all these items together.
My main requirement is to extract the UI Switches assigned to each Business Role, but so far I could not find any report that provides this information.
I reviewed standard reports such as:
All Business Roles
All Current Access Rights
Access Rights Change Log
However, none of them include UI Switches.
Has anyone implemented a similar requirement before?
Any guidance or best practice would be highly appreciated.
Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Configuring Automatic packing in Outbound Delivery
entry.content: What is the function module - if you want to debug packing determination issue during Outbound delivery automatic packing?

entry.title: insert only if not exist
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

Given the following definition:

 

CREATE TABLE verify(name varchar(50), condition text, error text);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ind_verify ON verify( name );

 

How do I make sure I insert the records only if the name is not present already, but if its present just do nothing and continue without error.

 

I want to populate this table every time I start the program and establish a good connection to the DB.

Is check subquery the only way?

If I do an insert right now I am getting an error about unique constraint violation.

 

Thank you.

 

Working with SAP ASE 16.1.

Also someone needs to add/fix the appropriate tag.

When I type "SAP ASE" I see: "SAP ASE - BW Enablement" SAP ASE - ERP Enablement", "SAP ASE - HADR Enablement" and "SAP HANA accelerator for SAP ASE". What I don't see is "Adaptive Server Enterprise".

Thx.

entry.title: Re: Standard Access Sequence
entry.content:
Hello @Myselfsantanu 

Please find comments below ,

1. JGSO access Sequence is SAP India Standard or customised --> Yes SAP has delivered notes which we need to implement also shared documents , Please check https://me.sap.com/notes/2434329 , you will find the configuration changes and step by step procedure . Have attached PDF for your reference.

2. the said 4 condition table is SAP India standard which is automatically fetched in V/07 against JGSO or these are also manually created.  --> Part of the SAP Note and package implemented with GST 

3. Is there any Table to check the Created by User name for Condition table, Access sequence and Condition table?--> For Condition Table you can check in Transaction SE11 --> Enter the Condition Table and click on Display --> Under Attributes you will find the user name who has created but as mentioned these are part of SAP notes and packages which are implemented for GST - J1I_GST_LO

 

Regards,

Sidhesh Sathyabhama .

entry.title: Re: FRB1 Value is not updating in ME21N - Standard PO
entry.content: I kept FRB1 to enter manual amount. When I put 1000 as amount and press enter, the amount is not updating in condition value column.

entry.title: How to enable proxy management feature
entry.content:
Login in instance > Navigate to proxy management >Scroll down > Enable Advanced proxy management (Proxy now and proxy import) > Will get Proxy now feature in home page
Another way
Go to Settings > Click on proxy > Click on Become proxy >Proxy any username

entry.title: Re: Intern Conversion to Regular/ FT Employee
entry.content: Have you tried an Crossboarding event?

entry.title: Re: How to add translatable display texts to Configuration UI
entry.content: The translations need to be based on the user legal entity or User language in SF?

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement Date in Closed Period
entry.content:
Hello!

In bank statement processing the posting date of the accounting documents is derived from the bank statement date: your statement date = 11.12.2025

A practical solution will be to change posting date during reprocessing.  When you reprocess bank statement items:

  1. Open Reprocess Bank Statement Items ->  select the line item ->  choose:  Edit / Change
  2.  Manually override posting date, change from 11.12.2025 to desired date.

The outcome will be document is posted in current open period, statement date stays historically correct and no need to reopen period.

 

Alternative option is to  use “value date / entry date” (if configured), some setups allow posting date derived from entry date or value date. If your bank provides these fields correctly you can map posting date away from statement date.

 

Important consideration when you post in current open period:

  • Financial posting date = current open period
  • Bank statement date = Dec 2025

entry.title: Re: insert only if not exist
entry.content:
re:tags ...

Instead of searching for 'ASE', try searching for 'Adaptive' (NOTE: this question has already been tagged with 'SAP Adaptive Server  Enterprise'.)

I have no idea why SAP has not provided a tag named 'SAP ASE'.

entry.title: Re: Question: Bulk onboarding for acquired team members (...
entry.content: @ sarapatzelt98 - They don't receive the paper work even after the PDC is completed?

entry.title: Re: Closing Journal Entries Are Missing in Generated e-Le...
entry.content:
Hello!

External document types for the e-Ledger are given by GIB and must be defined in the "Specify External Document Type" configuration activity, and then SAP document types must be mapped in the separate "Assign SAP Document Type to External Document Type" activity. Even if "ZK" is maintained at the FI level, if it is not explicitly mapped to a GIB external document type in the e-Ledger configuration, the report engine will silently ignore those journal entries when building the TXT file.

Specify External Document Type, confirm the GIB compliant external type for year-end closing exists. Assign SAP Document Type to External Document Type, confirm document type ZK is explicitly mapped here.

Another reason could be that the special/closing period is not included in the report run. Year-end closing entries in SAP are typically posted to special periods (period 13, 14, 15, or 16), not period 12. The e-Ledger run for December 2025 may only be selecting fiscal period 12 without including the special closing periods. When executing the report in Run Statutory Reports, verify whether the selection parameters include special periods. If your closing entries are posted with a special period, the report must be explicitly parameterized to capture them.

Since this is a productive system with a GIB legal deadline, you should simultaneously open an SAP support case.

entry.title: Re: ONB2.0 - Rehired by a different company?
entry.content: New User ID linked to the same person

entry.title: Re: position- business relationship and job relationship...
entry.content: You will need to create a "calculated aggregated column" and filter by relationship type.

entry.title: Re: Sales Doc type for Rev Accruals
entry.content:
Hello!

Using the configure standard order type activity in CBC,  you can create a new custom sales order type, for example ZRA (or any name that makes sense), by copying the standard OR template. This automatically brings across all configuration settings tied to OR so you are not starting from scratch.

Once deployed to the system, this new type behaves like a standard sales order type. Can be customised specifically for needed revenue accrual process, for example, making it non-billing relevant so the orders never appear in billing runs, assigning separate number ranges so they are easy to identify in reporting, and adjusting pricing or account determination if accrual postings follow a different logic than standard sales orders.

The end result is a clean separation between revenue accrual orders and billing orders, which directly eliminates the manual filtering work.

One thing to confirm before is whether the system is currently on SAP CBC or still on the older Manage Your Solution configuration tool. This determines whether the capability is available or requires a short migration step first.

Below is a useful link about create custom sales order types in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/create-custom-sales-order-types-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/14349066

entry.title: Re: Detailed export of group permissions including exclus...
entry.content:
@jholmes09 
There is a Partner App that can do this 
https://sfcodebot.com/

entry.title: Re: Applicant notes, flags, or attachments that live on p...
entry.content:
The fields will need to be configured on the candidate template instead of Job application template.

most probably you will need custom field.

Try also to see the new candidate experience UI from Recruiter prespective and Candidate prespective

entry.title: Re: insert only if not exist
entry.content:
There are a few ways to do what you want ...

Assuming table is empty, we'll start with an initial insert:

insert verify (name, condition, error)
values ('name1', 'good condition', 'good error')
go

For each approach we'll perform two inserts:

  1. one insert for the previous name (eg, 'name1') with 'bad condition' and 'bad error'; no insert should be performed
  2. one for the next name (eg, 'name2') with 'good condition' and 'good error'; an insert should be performed

insert / select / where not exists(...)

-- should NOT insert

insert verify (name, condition, error)
select 'name1', 'bad condition', 'bad error'
where not exists(select name from verify where name = 'name1')
go

-- should insert

insert verify (name, condition, error)
select 'name2', 'good condition', 'good error'
where not exists(select name from verify where name = 'name2')
go

if not exists(...) / insert

-- should NOT insert

if not exists(select name from verify where name = 'name2')
begin
        insert verify (name, condition, error)
        values ('name2', 'bad condition', 'bad error')
end
go

-- should insert

if not exists(select name from verify where name = 'name3')
begin
        insert verify (name, condition, error)
        values ('name3', 'good condition', 'good error')
end
go

merge / when not matched

-- should NOT insert

merge into verify as tgt
      using (select 'name3','bad condition', 'bad error') as src (name,condition,error)
      on tgt.name = src.name
      when not matched
      then insert (name,condition,error)
           values (src.name, src.condition, src.error)
go

-- should insert

merge into verify as tgt
      using (select 'name4','good condition', 'good error') as src (name,condition,error)
      on tgt.name = src.name
      when not matched
      then insert (name,condition,error)
           values (src.name, src.condition, src.error)
go

At this point we should have 4 rows in our table, all with 'good condition' and 'good error' entries [NOTE: output has been edited to remove excessive spacing]:

select *
from verify
order by name
go

 name  condition      error
 ----- -------------- ----------
 name1 good condition good error
 name2 good condition good error
 name3 good condition good error
 name4 good condition good error

Obviously these example are basic T-SQL syntax.  I can't speak to how easy (or hard) these are to implement if using a programmed front end (eg, python, perl,), let alone how to handle any special characters that may be embedded in the values of the two text fields.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports Java Runtime: Fonts not correctly loa...
entry.content: They agreed and will look into adding your request in the next release, I think it will be sometime in December.....

entry.title: Re: 100558 - Map Format Data for Payments: User-Defined F...
entry.content:
Hello!

FPAYHX is the SAP internal structure called "Payment Medium: Prepared Data for Payment." It acts as a container that holds all payment related data before it is written into the output XML file by the DME (Data Medium Exchange) engine.

REF02 is a standard field within SAP Structure FPAYHX that stores user defined field information. The REF fields (REF01 through REF15) content is entirely format-specific, meaning SAP has decided what data goes into each REF slot differently for each payment format tree (CGI_CT, CH_CGI_XML_CT, DE_CGI_XML_CT, etc.).

REF fields (FPAYHX-REF01 through REF15) are format specific.  When FPAYHX-REF02 is used as the source for <BldgNb> in the CH_CGI_XML_CT tree, it does not mean REF02 equals building number, it means SAP has programmatically loaded the building number value into REF02 during event execution.

SAP Note 2513995 contains an Excel file that covers the mapping of all reference fields in the generic and country-specific CGI trees.

  • FPAYHX-REF02 means what data is loaded into it for the CH format (likely the creditor building number extracted from the vendor address)
  • FPAYHX-REF14 means what condition data is stored (likely a flag or indicator used to decide whether the structured or unstructured address format applies)

When you see something like FPAYHX-REF02+010(10) in the Excel or in the format tree, this means the REF02 field is a long string field and the data you want starts at offset 10 (character position 11) and is 10 characters long. Multiple different data values can be packed into a single REF field using different offsets. This is how SAP fits many pieces of information into the 15 REF slots available.

The fact that FPAYHX-REF14 appears in the condition tab (not the Source tab) for <BldgNb> means in the DME format tree, a condition controls whether a node is output at all. So REF14 is being used as a switch, it likely contains a flag value that indicates whether the creditor address is in structured format (with separate StrNm + BldgNb) or unstructured format (with AdrLine entries). The CGI format supports both structured address (with separate <StrtNm>, <BldgNb>, <PstCd>, <TwnNm>, <Ctry>) and unstructured address (with <AdrLine> entries).

On Public Cloud exist no no access to OBPM1/SE11, the relevant event logic is handled internally by SAP's standard class and its country-specific methods. Can’t be modifed in Public Cloud, the values are populated automatically by SAP from the vendor master address data when the payment run executes.

If <BldgNb> tag is not being populated in the output XML, the most likely reasons are:

  • The building number is not maintained separately in the vendor master address (it may be concatenated into the street name field instead)
  • The REF14 condition evaluates to false because the address format indicator is set to unstructured

entry.title: Re: Assistance With Crystal Report With Barcodes
entry.content:
A quick Google search indicates that UFL with that function belongs to IDAutomation.

You can go here to get it:

https://www.idautomation.com/font-encoders/crystal-reports/

entry.title: Re: Unable to send reports to external user emails
entry.content: Check with your IT department and see if they have changed permissions and anything

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content: @julieplummer20 what is the ETA for releasing the docker image?

entry.title: Re: Shared module mdk-core/controls/BaseControl doesn't exist in shared scope default.
entry.content: I am also facing the same issue. Did you find any solution. I am using SSAM Metadata 2505.

entry.title: A Customer Perspective: Sequencing SuccessFactors “Latest” Experiences for Better Adoption
entry.content:
SuccessFactors continues to invest in modern user experiences—something customers generally support because it improves usability, accessibility, and long-term innovation. However, a pattern is emerging that many customers are now experiencing in real time: multiple “Latest” experiences are arriving in parallel, and the combined impact is creating unnecessary adoption risk.

Two major examples are happening around the same time for many organizations:

  • Latest Home Page experience
  • Latest Performance Form experience

To support adoption without compromising product direction, I propose adjusting the timelines for the impacted legacy performance form experience to give customers room to transition in a structured way.

Requested adjustments for Legacy Performance Form Experience:

  • End of Maintenance: move to May 2027
  • Deletion Date: move to November 2027

This provides two important benefits:

  1. Sequencing: customers can implement and stabilize the Latest Home Page first, then move to the Latest Performance Form experience based on their cycle timing.
  2. Change management quality: customers can run enablement properly—communications, training, pilot groups, updated job aids, and support readiness—rather than pushing two major UI changes into one compressed period.

    Seeing the same challenges? Add your vote to the Customer Influence request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#idea/374034

entry.title: Re: NYC SAP Community: First Meetup of 2026 — Sapphire Recap & New Faces Joining Us
entry.content: I am really happy to see Deise with you. Deise helps us a lot here in Brazil.

entry.title: Re: Standard Access Sequence
entry.content: Than you so much. I got my answer now.

entry.title: Re: New SAP Activate Roadmap for SAP Sales Cloud V2
entry.content:
@Chandan_Gurav what about for SAP Service Cloud V2 and ESM ?

Also for Utilities ?.. since Activate contains only for SAP Sales Cloud V2..
